relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: False entry marks thief and robber.
The true shepherd enters through the door.
The sheep hear the voice and are called by name.
The shepherd goes before, and known voice draws following.
Unknown voices cannot shepherd the flock.
They do not yet understand the figure.
Jesus reveals himself as the door of the sheep.
The sheep did not hear thief-voices.
Through the door are saving/healing, movement, and pasture.
Against theft and destruction, Jesus brings abundant life.
The good shepherd lays down life for the sheep.
The hired worker abandons the flock before the wolf.
Care is absent when the sheep do not matter to the worker.
The good shepherd knows and is known.
Father-Son knowing shapes shepherd-sheep knowing.
Other sheep will hear the voice; one flock, one shepherd.
The Father loves the self-giving life that can be taken again.
The laying down and taking up are held in authority and command.
The words create division.
Some dismiss him with spirit-trouble language.
Others hear that these words do not fit blindness-making power.
Dedication comes in Jerusalem’s winter.
Jesus walks in Solomon’s portico.
They demand open Messiah speech.
The Father’s works already bear witness.
Mistrust reveals non-belonging to the sheep.
His sheep hear, are known, and follow.
Life is given and no one seizes them from his hand.
The Father’s hand holds what has been given.
I and the Father are one.
Stones rise against oneness language.
Jesus points back to the Father’s good works.
They name the issue as human claiming divine relation.
Jesus answers from Scripture’s divine-ones language.
Scripture cannot be casually broken.
The sanctified and sent Son names his relation truthfully.
Works become the test of trust.
The works reveal mutual indwelling: Father in me, I in Father.
They seek to seize him, but he goes out from their hand.
He returns beyond the Jordan, to John’s first witness-place.
John did no sign, yet his witness was true.
Many trust there.
